title: Amount of substance (moles)
short: The number of particles counted in moles, where one mole is about 6.022 × 10²³ particles.
full:
The amount of substance n counts how many elementary entities (atoms, molecules) are present, in moles. One mole contains Avogadro's number, about 6.022 × 10²³, of them — a bookkeeping unit that turns vast particle counts into manageable numbers.

It appears in the ideal gas law P·V = n·R·T, where doubling the number of moles at fixed volume and temperature doubles the pressure.

The SI unit is the mole (mol).
